rationale: The cited 2001 paper appears methodologically central: it introduces Bayesian conjoint choice design (DB-error) and relabeling/swapping/cycling search heuristics, while the 2005 citing paper also focuses on Bayesian/mixed-logit conjoint design efficiency and uses swapping/cycling construction. The citing note explicitly frames its contribution as extending prior homogeneous conjoint design work, which plausibly includes this cited method source rather than a generic mention. With this direct topical and methodological overlap (and a DOI-verified reference match), this pair should be retained for full relation judgment.
